item.author: apinke

item.title: 使用“useState”创建步进器组件 | Creating A Stepper Component with `useState`
item.description:
我们以前都见过踏步机吧？ 不，不是模拟走楼梯的健身机。 步进器是一个 UI，它向您显示您在任务、问题、步骤等列表中的位置。 它们提供类似向导的工作流程，有时可以用作导航。 使用 React 和useState钩子也很容易创建它们。

坦白说，这个项目的想法不是我自己想出来的。 我仍在通过 Jonas Schmedtmann 的React 课程刷新我的 React 知识。 这是课程中的一个练习项目。 上周我写了一篇文章，详细介绍了如何从同一课程创建开发人员资料卡。 如果您有兴趣了解我是如何做到的，请务必查看！

我对这些项目所做的就是暂停视频，并尝试自己完成它们。 我发现这可以帮助我找出我已经知道的事情，同时迫使我挑战自己找出我不知道的事情。 如果您像我一样患有冒名顶替综合症，这是一个推荐的方法。 探索您自己的教程解决方案是测试和巩固您的知识的好方法。 让我们开始吧。

先决条件

在这篇文章中，我假设您具备 JavaScript 及其数组方法的应用知识。 我还假设您了解一些 React 基础知识，例如 JSX、如何创建组件以及如何传递 props。 在 React 中，props（属性的缩写）是一种将数据从父组件传递到子组件的方式。 它们允许我们的组件根据收到的输入进行不同的行为，从而使我们的组件更加通用和可重用。 如果您需要复习其中任何内容，我建议您查看以下资源：

开始使用步骤进度组件

让我们深入研究并使用 React 和 useState 创建我们自己的 Step Progress 组件。

设置项目

首先，我们需要建立一个新的 React 项目。 如果您还没有一个，您可以使用 Create React App 创建一个新的。

 npx create-react-app step-progress-component

我通常使用 Vite 来创建 React 项目，但由于本教程需要 CRA，所以我坚持使用它。

创建 React 项目后，导航到项目目录并启动本地开发服务器。

 cd step-progress-component npm start

现在，您应该在http://localhost:3000上运行一个新的 React 项目。

删除样板代码

在这种情况下，我们不需要App.css 、 App.test.js 、 reportWebVitals.js 、 setupTests.js ，也不需要logo.svg 。 在index.js中，删除并跟踪reportWebVitals 。 在App.js中删除App.css和logo.svg的导入。 然后删除第一个div之间的所有内容，并删除className="App" 。

我添加的只是按钮的.active样式，以及主体的额外样式以使其美观。

 * { box-sizing: border-box; } body { font-family: sans-serif; color: #333; min-height: 100vh; display: grid; place-content: center; background-image: radial-gradient(circle, #7950f2, #995ef5, #b36df8, #ca7dfb, #df8eff); } .steps { width: 600px; background-color: #f7f7f7; border-radius: 7px; padding: 25px 100px; margin: 100px auto; } .numbers { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; } .numbers > div { height: 40px; aspect-ratio: 1; background-color: #e7e7e7; border-radius: 50%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 18px; } .numbers .active, button.active { background-color: #7950f2; color: #fff; } .message { text-align: center; font-size: 20px; margin: 40px 0; font-weight: bold; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; } .buttons { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; } .buttons button { border: none; cursor: pointer; padding: 10px 15px; border-radius: 100px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: bold; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; } .buttons button span { font-size: 16px; line-height: 1; } h3 { margin: 0; text-transform: uppercase; }

创建步骤组件

首先，让我们深入了解我们的 Step 组件。 该组件接受两个属性：styleClass 和 number。

 export default function Step({ styleClass, number }) { return <div className={styleClass}>{number}</div>; }

这个组件非常简单，但它通过渲染进度条中的每个步骤在我们的应用程序中发挥着关键作用。

制作 StepText 组件

我们将单个text属性传递给该组件，然后在h3标签中进行渲染。

 export default function StepText({ text }) { return <h3>{text}</h3>; }

这个组件是 React 中“组合”原则的一个很好的例子，它使我们能够保持我们的组件小、集中且可重用。

按钮，按钮，谁有按钮？

接下来，我们将转到Button组件。 该组件将呈现我们的“下一步”和“上一步”按钮，并将处理在步骤之间移动的用户交互。

 export default function Button({ children, onClick, styleClass }) { return ( <button className={styleClass} onClick={onClick}> <span>{children}</span> </button> ); }

我们传递给按钮的onClick属性允许我们处理用户点击，从而操纵进度栏中的活动步骤。 将所有内容整合在一起

在App.js中，我们需要导入所有三个组件。

我建议将各个组件放置在src目录中的components文件夹中。 从长远来看，这有助于更好的组织。 由于这是一个较小的项目，我们可以不这样做。

使用useState添加交互性

准备好组件后，让我们继续创建交互式步进器。 为此，我们将使用 React 的useState钩子。 状态可以被认为是跟踪应用程序中信息的一种方式，这些信息会随着时间的推移而变化并影响应用程序的行为。

在我们的App.js中，我们首先从 React 导入useState和我们的三个组件： Step 、 StepText和Button 。

 import { useState } from 'react'; import Step from './Step'; import StepText from './StepText'; import Button from './Button';

然后，让我们为进度条的每个步骤定义文本。 我们将其存储在一个名为stepText的数组中。

 const stepText = ['Learn React ⚛️', 'Apply to jobs 💼', 'Get a job 🎉'];

为我们的步进器创建状态

接下来，我们将创建我们的step状态。 我们将其初始化为 1，因为我们想从第一步开始。 我们使用setStep来改变状态。

 const [step, setStep] = useState(1);

处理步骤变更

之后，我们将创建两个函数handleNext和handlePrevious 。 当单击下一个和上一个按钮时，这些函数将允许我们在进度栏中前进或后退。

 const handleNext = () => { if (step < stepText.length) { setStep(step + 1); } }; const handlePrevious = () => { if (step > 1) { setStep(step - 1); } };

渲染我们的组件

最后，我们将渲染我们的组件。 我们为每个步骤创建一个 div，并有条件地将“active”类应用于当前步骤。 我们还渲染当前步骤以及上一个和下一个按钮的文本，再次有条件地应用“活动”类。

 return ( <div className="steps"> <div className="numbers"> {stepText.map((_, index) => ( <Step styleClass={step === index + 1 ? "active" : ""} key={`Step ${index + 1}`} number={index + 1} /> ))} </div> <div className="message"> <StepText text={stepText[step - 1]} /> </div> <div className="buttons"> <Button styleClass={step > 1 ? "active" : ""} onClick={handlePrevious}> Previous </Button> <Button styleClass={step < stepText.length ? "active" : ""} onClick={handleNext} > Next </Button> </div> </div> );

就是这样！ 我们使用 React 和useState钩子构建了一个功能性的 Step Progress 组件。 结论

学习React就是要理解它的核心概念，useState绝对是其中之一。 通过构建这个 Step Progress 组件，我们不仅更好地了解了 useState 的工作原理，而且还学习了如何使用组件构建代码以及如何传递 props 以使它们更可重用。 这是成为一名更熟练的 React 开发人员的一大进步。 继续练习，你一定会成功的！

如果您想查看步进器的运行情况，请查看我的笔！



原文:

We've all seen a stepper before right? No, not the fitness machine that simulates walking up a flight of stairs. A stepper is a piece of UI that shows you where you are in a list of tasks, questions, steps, etc,. They provide a wizard-like workflow and can are sometimes used as navigation. They're also pretty easy to create with React and the useState hook.

Full disclosure, I didn't come up with the idea of this project on my own. I'm still refreshing my React knowledge with Jonas Schmedtmann's React course. This is a practice project from the course. Last week I wrote a post detailing how I created a developer profile card from the same course. If you're interested learning how I did it, be sure to check it out!

What I've been doing with these projects is pausing the video, and attempting to complete them on my own. I find this helps me find out what I already know while forcing me to challenge myself to figure out the things I don't. This is a recommended approach if you struggle with imposter syndrome like I do. Exploring your own solutions to tutorials is a great way to test and solidify your knowledge. Let's get started.

Prerequisites

In this post, I'm assuming you have a working knowledge of JavaScript and its array methods. I'm also assuming you know some React basics like JSX, how to create components, and how to pass props. In React, props (short for properties) are a way of passing data from parent components to child components. They make our components more versatile and reusable by allowing them to behave differently based on the input they receive. If you need a refresher on any of this, I recommended checking out the following resources:

Getting Started with the Step Progress Component

Let's dive in and create our own Step Progress Component with React and useState.

Setting up the Project

First, we need to set up a new React project. If you don't already have one, you can create a new one using Create React App.

npx create-react-app step-progress-component

I normally use Vite for creating React projects, but since this tutorial called for CRA, I stuck with it.

Once you've created your React project, navigate to the project directory and start your local development server.

cd step-progress-component
npm start

You should now have a new React project running on http://localhost:3000.

Remove boilerplate code

Create React App gives us a lot of code/files that we may not always need. In this case, we don't need App.css, App.test.js, reportWebVitals.js, setupTests.js, nor logo.svg. In index.js, remove and trace of reportWebVitals. In App.js remove the imports for App.css and logo.svg. Then remove everything between the first div, and delete className="App".

Jonas provided the CSS for this project, so we'll be replacing the code in index.css with that. All I added was the .active styling for buttons, and extra styling to the body to make it pretty.

* { box-sizing: border-box;
} body { font-family: sans-serif; color: #333; min-height: 100vh; display: grid; place-content: center; background-image: radial-gradient(circle, #7950f2, #995ef5, #b36df8, #ca7dfb, #df8eff);
} .steps { width: 600px; background-color: #f7f7f7; border-radius: 7px; padding: 25px 100px; margin: 100px auto;
} .numbers { display: flex; justify-content: space-between;
} .numbers > div { height: 40px; aspect-ratio: 1; background-color: #e7e7e7; border-radius: 50%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 18px;
} .numbers .active,
button.active { background-color: #7950f2; color: #fff;
} .message { text-align: center; font-size: 20px; margin: 40px 0; font-weight: bold; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center;
} .buttons { display: flex; justify-content: space-between;
} .buttons button { border: none; cursor: pointer; padding: 10px 15px; border-radius: 100px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: bold; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px;
} .buttons button span { font-size: 16px; line-height: 1;
} h3 { margin: 0; text-transform: uppercase;
}

Creating the Step component

Firstly, let's dive into our Step Component. This component accepts two props, styleClass and number.

export default function Step({ styleClass, number }) { return <div className={styleClass}>{number}</div>;
}

The styleClass prop allows us to apply conditional styling to our component depending on what step is active, and the number prop passes a number to the component based on the current step.

This component is quite simple, but it plays a key role in our application by rendering each step in the progress bar.

Crafting the StepText Component

The StepText component is responsible for displaying the text associated with each step in our progress bar. We pass a single text prop to this component, which we then render within an h3 tag.

export default function StepText({ text }) { return <h3>{text}</h3>;
}

This component is a fantastic example of the principle of "composition" in React, allowing us to keep our components small, focused, and reusable.

Button, button, who's got the button?

Next, we'll move onto the Button component. This component will render our 'Next' and 'Previous' buttons, and will handle user interactions to move between steps.

export default function Button({ children, onClick, styleClass }) { return ( <button className={styleClass} onClick={onClick}> <span>{children}</span> </button> );
}

The onClick prop that we pass to our button allows us to handle user clicks and thus manipulate the active step in our progress bar. The children prop allows us to pass the button text as children of the Button component.

Bringing it all together

In App.js, we need to import all three components.

I recommend placing individual components in a components folder in the src directory. This makes for better organization in the long run. Since this is a smaller project, we can get away with not doing this.

Adding Interactivity with useState

With our components ready, let's move on to creating an interactive stepper. For this, we'll be using React's useState hook. Hooks are a relatively new addition to React that allows us to use state and other React features in our functional components. useState is one of these hooks and it allows us to add state to our functional components. State can be thought of as a way to keep track of information in your application that can change over time and affect your app's behavior.

In our App.js, we'll start by importing useState from React and our three components: Step, StepText, and Button.

import { useState } from 'react';
import Step from './Step';
import StepText from './StepText';
import Button from './Button';

Then, let's define the text for each step of our progress bar. We'll store this in an array called stepText.

const stepText = ['Learn React ⚛️', 'Apply to jobs 💼', 'Get a job 🎉'];

Creating the State for Our Stepper

Next, we're going to create our step state. We initialize it to 1 since we want to start at the first step. We use setStep to change the state.

const [step, setStep] = useState(1);

Handling Step Changes

After that, we'll create two functions handleNext and handlePrevious. These functions will allow us to go forward or backward in our progress bar when the next and previous buttons are clicked.

const handleNext = () => { if (step < stepText.length) { setStep(step + 1); }
}; const handlePrevious = () => { if (step > 1) { setStep(step - 1); }
};

Rendering Our Components

Finally, we'll render our components. We create a div for each step and conditionally apply the 'active' class to the current step. We also render the text for the current step and the previous and next buttons, again applying the 'active' class conditionally.

return ( <div className="steps"> <div className="numbers"> {stepText.map((_, index) => ( <Step styleClass={step === index + 1 ? "active" : ""} key={`Step ${index + 1}`} number={index + 1} /> ))} </div> <div className="message"> <StepText text={stepText[step - 1]} /> </div> <div className="buttons"> <Button styleClass={step > 1 ? "active" : ""} onClick={handlePrevious}> Previous </Button> <Button styleClass={step < stepText.length ? "active" : ""} onClick={handleNext} > Next </Button> </div> </div>
);

And that's it! We've built a functional Step Progress Component using React and the useState hook. The full code for App.js looks like this:

import { useState } from "react";
import Step from "./components/Step";
import StepText from "./components/StepText";
import Button from "./components/Button"; const stepText = ["Learn React ⚛️", "Apply to jobs 💼", "Get a job 🎉"]; function App() { const [step, setStep] = useState(1); const handleNext = () => { if (step < stepText.length) { setStep(step + 1); } console.log(step); }; const handlePrevious = () => { if (step > 1) { setStep(step - 1); } console.log(step); }; return ( <div className="steps"> <div className="numbers"> {stepText.map((_, index) => ( <Step styleClass={step === index + 1 ? "active" : ""} key={`Step ${index + 1}`} number={index + 1} /> ))} </div> <div className="message"> <StepText text={stepText[step - 1]} /> </div> <div className="buttons"> <Button styleClass={step > 1 ? "active" : ""} onClick={handlePrevious}> Previous </Button> <Button styleClass={step < stepText.length ? "active" : ""} onClick={handleNext} > Next </Button> </div> </div> );
} export default App;

Conclusion

Learning React is all about understanding its core concepts, and useState is definitely one of them. By building this Step Progress Component, not only did we gain a better understanding of how useState works, but we also learned how to structure our code using components and how to pass props to make them more reusable. This is a great step forward in becoming a more proficient React developer. Keep practicing and you'll surely get there!

If you want to see the stepper in action, check out my pen!
item.author: ritabradley_dev

item.title: 使用 Amazon ECR Docker Credential Helper 向 AWS ECR 注册表验证您的 GitLab CI 运行程序 🔑 | Authenticating your GitLab CI runner to an AWS ECR registry using Amazon ECR Docker Credential Helper 🔑
item.description:
为了获得最大的灵活性，您可能需要从根据项目的特定需求定制的自行创建的 Docker 映像来运行作业。 您可以将此自行创建的私有 Docker 映像存储在 AWS ECR 注册表中。 在本教程中，我将解释如何使用 Amazon ECR Docker Credential Helper 设置从 GitLab 运行程序到注册表的自动身份验证。

亚搏体育appGitLab CI工作

创建一个 GitLab CI 作业，该作业使用保存在私有 AWS ECR 注册表中的 Docker 映像：

 phpunit: stage: testing image: name: 123456789123.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/php-gitlabrunner:latest entrypoint: [""] script: - php ./vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-text --colors=never

创建并配置您的运行程序以访问 AWS ECR 注册表

environment = ["DOCKER_AUTH_CONFIG={ \"credsStore\": \"ecr-login\" }"]

现在，您的 GitLab 运行程序可以自动向您的 ECR 注册表进行身份验证 🙂

如果您喜欢这篇文章，您可以在我的博客adrien-mornet.tech/ 🚀 上找到更多信息

一旦暂停，aws-builders 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦取消暂停，aws-builders 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aws-builders 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aws-builders 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 aws-builders：

使 aws-builders 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 aws-builders 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

GitLab CI allows you to run your CI/CD jobs in separate and isolated Docker containers. For maximum flexibility, you may need to run your jobs from a self-created Docker image tailored to your project’s specific needs. You can store this self-created and private Docker image in an AWS ECR registry. In this tutorial I will explain how to set up automatic authentication from your GitLab runner to your registry with Amazon ECR Docker Credential Helper.

GitLab CI job

Create a GitLab CI job which uses your Docker image saved in a private AWS ECR registry :

phpunit:
  stage: testing
  image: 
    name: 123456789123.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/php-gitlabrunner:latest
    entrypoint: [""]
  script:
    - php ./vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-text --colors=never

Create and configure your runner to access AWS ECR registry 

environment = ["DOCKER_AUTH_CONFIG={ \"credsStore\": \"ecr-login\" }"]

Now your GitLab runner can automatically authenticate to your ECR registry 🙂

If you liked this post, you can find more on my blog adrien-mornet.tech/ 🚀

Once suspended, aws-builders will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, aws-builders will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by aws-builders will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If aws-builders is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag aws-builders:

Make all posts by aws-builders less visible

aws-builders consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging aws-builders will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: adrienmornet

item.title: 与OpenAi(ChatGPT)集成(附代码示例) | Integrating With OpenAi(ChatGPT)(With Code example)
item.description:
穆罕默德·阿里·阿卜杜勒瓦赫德

发表于 6 月 23 日

如果您的项目要求需要将其与 OpenAi 集成，这里根据 OpenAi 文档简要介绍如何做到这一点：首先，您需要明确您的需求，因为 OpenAi 依赖于许多模型和引擎，每个模型和引擎都是一个人工智能模型根据特定数量的数据进行训练，可以提供有限数量的单词或类型的响应。 根据响应类型，它分为 2 个主要类别： 完成：在这种类型中，用法将用于完成和您发送的文本，作为示例：text-davinci-001、text-davinci-002、text -达芬奇-003

最后的数字代表响应的准确度，达芬奇3最高。

聊天：在这种类型中，用法将用于聊天，它的工作原理与您使用 Chatgpt 聊天 100% 相同，作为其型号的示例：gpt-3.5-turbo、gpt-3.5-turbo-0613 ..etc

注意：请注意，准确性越高，响应时间就会越长。

雅库布·安杰耶夫斯基 - 6 月 19 日

一旦被暂停，maliano63717738 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，maliano63717738 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，maliano63717738 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 maliano63717738 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 maliano63717738：

使 maliano63717738 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 maliano63717738 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Muhammed ALi Abdulwahed

Posted on Jun 23

 

If your project's requirement puts you in need of integrating it with OpenAi here is a brief depending on OpenAi documentation of how to do that : First, you need to make your needs clear because OpenAi depends on many models and engines each one of them is an Ai model trained on a specific amount of data and can provide a limited amount of words or types of responses. Based on the type of response it goes for 2 main categories : Completion: In this type, the usage will be for the completing and text you send and as an example of it : text-davinci-001,text-davinci-002,text-davinci-003

the number in the end represent the accuracy of the response,davinci 3 is the highest.

Chat: In this type, the usage will be for the Chat and it works 100% the same as you are chatting with Chatgpt, as an example of its models : gpt-3.5-turbo,gpt-3.5-turbo-0613 ..etc

gpt-3.5-turbo-0613 is the newest one and the one with the highest accuracy,code example:


Note: please note that as much as you raise your accuracy the response time will be higher.

Jakub Andrzejewski - Jun 19

item.title: Pitampura 即时牙齿美白终极指南 | The Ultimate Guide to Instant Teeth Whitening in Pitampura
item.description:
欢迎来到我们关于在 Pitampura 实现即时牙齿美白的综合指南。 如果您想增强笑容并增强自信，这篇文章就是您的首选资源。 我们了解拥有灿烂笑容的重要性，我们随时为您提供专家提示和见解，帮助您实现令人垂涎的珍珠白微笑。

了解即时牙齿美白

近年来，即时牙齿美白变得越来越流行，因为它提供了一种快速便捷的解决方案来实现更灿烂的笑容。 与可能需要数周或数月才能显示结果的传统美白方法不同，即时牙齿美白治疗可提供立竿见影的效果。

即时牙齿美白的好处

你可以走进一家牙科诊所，几个小时内就能带着更白皙的笑容走出去。

另一方面，即时牙齿美白治疗通常只需一次就诊即可完成，从而节省您的时间和麻烦。

即时牙齿美白可以帮助您对个人和职业生活更加自信，给您遇到的人留下持久的印象。

即时牙齿美白的过程

现在，让我们深入探讨Pitampura即时牙齿美白的过程：

第一步：咨询

第一步是安排与专门从事即时牙齿美白的值得信赖的牙科专家进行咨询。 在这次咨询期间，牙医将评估您的口腔健康状况，讨论您的期望，并推荐最适合您的美白治疗方案。

第二步：准备

在美白程序开始之前，牙医将确保您的牙齿清洁且没有任何牙菌斑或牙垢。 他们可能会进行彻底的清洁，以去除表面污渍和碎屑，使美白剂更有效地发挥作用。

第三步：应用美白剂

牙医会小心地在您的牙齿上涂抹专业级的美白凝胶或溶液。 这种强效配方含有漂白剂，可分解顽固污渍和变色，展现更灿烂的笑容。 在某些情况下，可以使用专门的光或激光来激活美白剂并加速该过程。

第四步：监控

在整个治疗过程中，牙医将密切监控美白过程的进展，以确保获得最佳效果。 他们可能会根据您的个人需求和偏好调整治疗的强度或持续时间。

第五步：美白后护理

一旦达到所需的美白程度，牙医将为您提供术后护理说明。 这些措施可能包括避免某些可能弄脏牙齿的食物和饮料，保持良好的口腔卫生习惯，以及安排定期牙科检查以延长美白治疗的效果。

保持更白皙的笑容

为了保持您新获得的洁白笑容，以下是一些有用的提示：

可能会导致染色的酱汁。

戒烟有助于保持更灿烂的笑容。

结论

总之，Pitampura 的即时牙齿美白为实现更洁白的笑容提供了便捷有效的解决方案。 通过遵循推荐的程序并保持良好的口腔卫生，您可以享受灿烂笑容的好处，增强您的信心并留下持久的印象。 不要再等待了，立即与 Pitampura 信誉良好的牙科专家预约咨询，释放您微笑的潜力！



原文:

Welcome to our comprehensive guide on achieving instant teeth whitening in Pitampura. If you're looking to enhance your smile and boost your confidence, this article is your go-to resource. We understand the importance of having a bright and radiant smile, and we're here to provide you with expert tips and insights to help you achieve that coveted pearly white smile.

Understanding Instant Teeth Whitening

Instant teeth whitening has become increasingly popular in recent years, as it offers a quick and convenient solution to achieve a brighter smile. Unlike traditional whitening methods that may take weeks or months to show results, instant teeth whitening treatments provide immediate and noticeable outcomes.

The Benefits of Instant Teeth Whitening

1. Immediate Results: One of the key advantages of instant teeth whitening is the instant gratification it offers. You can walk into a dental clinic and walk out with a whiter smile in a matter of hours.

2. Convenience: Traditional teeth whitening methods often require multiple visits to the dentist and the use of cumbersome trays or strips. Instant teeth whitening treatments, on the other hand, are typically completed in a single visit, saving you time and hassle.

3. Enhanced Confidence: A bright and beautiful smile can do wonders for your self-esteem. Instant teeth whitening can help you feel more confident in your personal and professional life, leaving a lasting impression on those you meet.

The Process of Instant Teeth Whitening

Now, let's delve into the process of instant teeth whitening in Pitampura:

Step 1: Consultation

The first step is to schedule a consultation with a trusted dental professional who specializes in instant teeth whitening. During this consultation, the dentist will evaluate your oral health, discuss your expectations, and recommend the most suitable whitening treatment for you.

Step 2: Preparation

Before the whitening procedure begins, the dentist will ensure your teeth are clean and free from any plaque or tartar. They may perform a thorough cleaning to remove surface stains and debris, allowing the whitening agent to work more effectively.

Step 3: Application of Whitening Agent

The dentist will carefully apply a professional-grade whitening gel or solution to your teeth. This powerful formula contains bleaching agents that break down stubborn stains and discoloration, revealing a brighter smile. In some cases, a specialized light or laser may be used to activate the whitening agent and accelerate the process.

Step 4: Monitoring

Throughout the treatment, the dentist will closely monitor the progress of the whitening process to ensure optimal results. They may adjust the intensity or duration of the treatment based on your individual needs and preferences.

Step 5: Post-Whitening Care

Once the desired level of whitening has been achieved, the dentist will provide you with aftercare instructions. These may include avoiding certain food and drinks that can stain your teeth, maintaining good oral hygiene practices, and scheduling regular dental check-ups to prolong the effects of the whitening treatment.

Maintaining Your Whiter Smile

To maintain your newly achieved white smile, here are some helpful tips:

1. Practice Good Oral Hygiene: Brush your teeth at least twice a day with a whitening toothpaste and floss regularly to remove plaque and prevent stains.

2. Limit Staining Foods and Drinks: Avoid or minimize consumption of dark-colored beverages such as coffee, tea, and red wine, as well as foods like berries and tomato
sauce that can cause staining.

3. Use a Straw: When drinking beverages that may stain your teeth, consider using a straw to minimize contact between the liquid and your teeth.

4. Quit Smoking: Smoking not only affects your overall health but also contributes to tooth discoloration. Quitting smoking can help maintain a brighter smile.

Conclusion

In conclusion, instant teeth whitening in Pitampura provides a convenient and effective solution for achieving a whiter smile. By following the recommended procedures and practicing good oral hygiene, you can enjoy the benefits of a radiant smile that boosts your confidence and leaves a lasting impression. Don't wait any longer—schedule a consultation with a reputable dental professional in Pitampura and unlock the potential of your smile today!
item.author: adityasharma23

item.title: 简而言之，坚实的原则 | SOLID Principles in a Nutshell
item.description:
我认为所有软件工程师都应该理解的最重要的一组原则是 SOLID 原则（不是唯一的，而是最基本的）。 通过理解这一原则，我们假设我们将能够提供良好、简洁且可维护的代码。

这是使用面向对象编程开发程序的最佳实践。

有很多文章对此进行了讨论。 但我认为这仍然是一个值得重新讨论的话题。 让我们尝试以最简单的方式讨论这个问题。 本文还包括 Java 示例代码。

单一责任原则

这一原则的主要目标是单个类应该只负责一个目的。 这意味着如果我们创建一个包含多个用途的类，我们将违反这一原则。

遵循这个原则，会给我们带来好处：

  • 我们的代码将更加模块化。

  • 这可以让我们更容易地在将来跟踪我们的代码，因为我们已经确定了每个类的具体用途。

错误的例子

public class Person { private String firstname; private String lastname; private String origin; private String age; private String sex; //constructor, getters and setters // methods that directly relate to the person properties public String returnFullName() { return firstname + " " + lastname; } }

然后我们添加storeInDatabase方法。

 public class Person { private String firstname; private String lastname; private String origin; private String age; private String sex; //constructor, getters and setters // methods that directly relate to the person properties public String returnFullName() { return firstname + " " + lastname; } public void storeInDatabase(Person person) { // codes that perform database action } }

出了什么问题：

  • 正确的例子

    为了解决这个问题，我们可以将storeInDatabase方法放在一个新类中，比如PersonDatabase类。

     public class PersonDatabase { public void storeInDatabase(Person person) { // codes that perform database action } }

    开闭原则

    这个原则强调类中应该对扩展开放但对修改封闭。

    遵循这个原则的好处是我们不会修改现有的代码，这可能会导致新的错误。 例子

    假设我们有如下的Car类。

     public class Car { private String brand; private String color; // constructors, getters, and setters }

    然后，我们想要一个与Car类具有相同属性的Truck类，并添加了一些内容。 我们可以将其扩展为Truck类，而不是修改Car类，如下所示。

     public class Truck extends Car { private int cargoVolume; // constructors, getters, and setters }

    通过这样做，我们不需要修改可能已经在程序的某些部分中使用的Car类。

    如果您想将来创建另一个类（例如Ambulance类），您可以像扩展Truck类一样扩展Car类。

     public class Ambulance extends Car { private hasbloodPressureGague; private hasThermometer; // constructors, getters, and setters }

    里氏替换原则

    这个原则可以说是这 5 个原则中最复杂的原则。 这个原则的简单含义可能是子类应该可以替代它们的基类。

    可以说， Toyota类是Car类的子类。 显示这个例子：

     class Car { private String brand; public Car(String brand) { this.brand = brand; } public void startEngine() { System.out.println("Engine started."); } public void stopEngine() { System.out.println("Engine stopped."); } } class Toyota extends Car { public Toyota() { super("Toyota"); } @Override public void startEngine() { System.out.println("Toyota engine started."); } @Override public void stopEngine() { System.out.println("Toyota engine stopped."); } public void engageAutopilot() { System.out.println("Autopilot engaged."); } }

    在此 Java 示例中， Car类表示通用汽车， Toyota类表示扩展基类行为的特定类型汽车（丰田品牌）。

    我们可以互换使用Car类和Toyota类，如下所示：

     public class Main { public static void startCar(Car car) { car.startEngine(); } public static void stopCar(Car car) { car.stopEngine(); } public static void engageAutopilot(Car car) { if (car instanceof Toyota) { Toyota toyota = (Toyota) car; toyota.engageAutopilot(); } else { System.out.println("Autopilot not available for this car."); } } public static void main(String[] args) { Car myCar = new Car("SomeBrand"); startCar(myCar); // Output: Engine started. stopCar(myCar); // Output: Engine stopped. Toyota myToyota = new Toyota(); startCar(myToyota); // Output: Toyota engine started. stopCar(myToyota); // Output: Toyota engine stopped. engageAutopilot(myCar); // Output: Autopilot not available for this car. engageAutopilot(myToyota); // Output: Autopilot engaged. } }

    我们可以将Car实例 ( myCar ) 和Toyota实例 ( myToyota ) 传递给这些方法，不会出现任何问题。

    如果是，它将其转换为Toyota对象并调用engageAutopilot()方法。 否则，它会处理自动驾驶功能不适用于非Toyota汽车的情况。 这演示了在不违反里氏替换原则的情况下将Toyota用作Car类的子类型。

    接口隔离原则

    隔离意味着将不同的事物分开。 该原则是根据接口的用途来分离接口。

    该原则指出，许多特定于客户端的接口比一个通用接口更好。 不应强迫客户实现他们不需要的功能。

     interface Worker { void work(); void sleep(); }

    假设我们有 2 个实现Worker接口的类， Programmer和Robot类。

     class Programmer implements Worker { public void work() { System.out.println("Programmer is working."); // Perform programming tasks } public void sleep() { System.out.println("Programmer is sleeping."); // Sleep at night } } class Robot implements Worker { public void work() { System.out.println("Robot is working."); // Perform robotic tasks } // Can't be implemented public void sleep() { // Robots don't sleep! throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Robots don't sleep!"); } }

    正如我们所看到的，sleep()方法可以由Programmer类实现，但不能由Robot类实现。 为了解决这个问题，我们可以拆分Worker接口。

     interface Worker { void work(); } interface LifeBeing { void sleep(); }

    然而， Robot类只实现了Worker接口，因为它不需要休眠。

     class Programmer implements Worker, LifeBeing { public void work() { System.out.println("Programmer is working."); // Perform programming tasks } public void sleep() { System.out.println("Programmer is sleeping."); // Sleep at night } } class Robot implements Worker { public void work() { System.out.println("Robot is working."); // Perform robotic tasks } }

    依赖倒置原则

    这个原则是指软件模块的解耦。 依赖倒置是面向对象编程中的一个设计原则，它指出高级模块不应该依赖于低级模块。 相反，两者都应该依赖于抽象。

     // High-level module class EmployeeTracker { private EmployeeService employeeService; public WeatherTracker(EmployeeService employeeService) { this.employeeService = employeeService; } public String getCurrentWeather() { int zipCode = 12345; return weatherService.getWeather(zipCode); } } // Abstraction (interface) for employee service interface EmployeeService { String getBranch(String employeeId); String getWage(String employeeId); String getPosition(String employeeId); } // Low-level module implementing the employee service class EmployeeServiceImpl implements EmployeeService { public String getBranch(String employeeId) { // Logic to retrieve employee data return employee.getBranch(); } public String getWage(String employeeId) { // Logic to retrieve employee data return employee.getWage(); } public String getPosition(String employeeId) { // Logic to retrieve employee data return employee.getPosition(); } }

    名为EmployeeTracker的高级模块需要检索员工数据。 它不直接依赖于具体实现，而是依赖于EmployeeService抽象/接口。

    它包含检索员工数据的具体实现细节，例如进行 API 调用。

    通过应用这个原则，我们颠倒了高层模块和低层模块之间的依赖关系。 高层模块依赖于抽象，而低层模块则依赖于这些抽象。 这促进了模块化和灵活的代码设计。

    结论

    这就是 SOLID 原则的简短解释。 您可以通过将这些原则应用到代码中来提高代码质量。 遵循这些原则我们将获得以下几个好处：

    • 可维护性：更容易维护和修改代码。

    • 可扩展性：无需修改现有代码即可添加新功能的能力。

    • 可测试性：促进有效的单元测试和代码验证。

    • 可重用性：创建可重用和可互换的组件。

    • 灵活性：更强的适应性和切换实施的能力。

    • 可读性和可理解性：清晰、有组织的代码结构，更容易理解和协作。

    其他资源



原文:

I think one of the most important sets of principles that should be understood by all software engineers is SOLID principles (not the only one, but the most basic). By understanding this principle, we assume that we'll be able to deliver good, cleaner and maintainable codes.

SOLID is an acronym that stands for Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, and Dependency Inversion. It's a best practice for developing a program using object-oriented programming.

There are so many articles that have discussed it. But, I think it is still a worthy topic to rediscuss about. Let's try to discuss this in the easiest way possible. This article also includes example codes in Java.

Single Responsibility Principle

The main goal of this principle is a single class should only responsible for one purpose. That means we will violate this principle if we create a class that consists of multiple purposes.

By following this principle, it will give us benefits:

  • Our code will be more modular.

  • This can be easier for us to trace our code in the future as we already determine the specific purpose of each class.

Wrong example 

public class Person { private String firstname; private String lastname; private String origin; private String age; private String sex; //constructor, getters and setters // methods that directly relate to the person properties public String returnFullName() { return firstname + " " + lastname; }
}

Then we add the storeInDatabase method.

public class Person { private String firstname; private String lastname; private String origin; private String age; private String sex; //constructor, getters and setters // methods that directly relate to the person properties public String returnFullName() { return firstname + " " + lastname; } public void storeInDatabase(Person person) { // codes that perform database action }
}

What goes wrong :

  • storeInDatabase is not directly related to or responsible for Person class.

Correct example

To fix this, we can put storeInDatabase method in a new class, let say PersonDatabase class.

public class PersonDatabase { public void storeInDatabase(Person person) { // codes that perform database action }
}

Open-Closed Principle

This principle emphasizes in a class should be open for extension but closed for modification.

The benefit of following this principle is we don't modify the existing code which can probably cause new bugs. (But, it's an exception for bugs in existing code, you should fix it, not extend it 😁).

Example

Suppose that we have Car class like below.

public class Car { private String brand; private String color; // constructors, getters, and setters
}

Then, we want a Truck class that has the same properties with Car class, with some additions. Instead of modifying Car class, we can extend it to Truck class like below.

public class Truck extends Car { private int cargoVolume; // constructors, getters, and setters
}

By doing so, we don't need to modify the Car class that might be already used in some part of our program.

If you want to create another class like Ambulance class in the future, you can extend the Car class like you did with Truck class.

public class Ambulance extends Car { private hasbloodPressureGague; private hasThermometer; // constructors, getters, and setters
}

Liskov Substitution Principle

This principle is arguably the most complex principle among these 5 principles. The simple meaning of this principle may be a subclasses should be substitutable for their base classes.

Let say, Toyota class is a subclass of Car class. Toyota class should be able to replace Car class without disrupting the behavior of our program. Show this example :

class Car { private String brand; public Car(String brand) { this.brand = brand; } public void startEngine() { System.out.println("Engine started."); } public void stopEngine() { System.out.println("Engine stopped."); }
} class Toyota extends Car { public Toyota() { super("Toyota"); } @Override public void startEngine() { System.out.println("Toyota engine started."); } @Override public void stopEngine() { System.out.println("Toyota engine stopped."); } public void engageAutopilot() { System.out.println("Autopilot engaged."); }
}

In this Java example, the Car class represents a generic car and a Toyota class represents a specific type of car (Toyota brand) that extends the behavior of the base class.

We can use the Car class and the Toyota class interchangeably, as demonstrated below:

public class Main { public static void startCar(Car car) { car.startEngine(); } public static void stopCar(Car car) { car.stopEngine(); } public static void engageAutopilot(Car car) { if (car instanceof Toyota) { Toyota toyota = (Toyota) car; toyota.engageAutopilot(); } else { System.out.println("Autopilot not available for this car."); } } public static void main(String[] args) { Car myCar = new Car("SomeBrand"); startCar(myCar); // Output: Engine started. stopCar(myCar); // Output: Engine stopped. Toyota myToyota = new Toyota(); startCar(myToyota); // Output: Toyota engine started. stopCar(myToyota); // Output: Toyota engine stopped. engageAutopilot(myCar); // Output: Autopilot not available for this car. engageAutopilot(myToyota); // Output: Autopilot engaged. }
}

The startCar() and stopCar() methods accept a Car object as a parameter. We can pass both a Car instance (myCar) and a Toyota instance (myToyota) to these methods without any issues.

The engageAutopilot() method checks if the provided Car object is an instance of Toyota. If it is, it casts it to a Toyota object and calls the engageAutopilot() method. Otherwise, it handles the case where autopilot functionality is not available for non-Toyota cars. This demonstrates the usage of the Toyota class as a subtype of the Car class without violating the Liskov substitution principle.

Interface Segregation Principle

Segregation means keeping different things separate. This principle is about separating interfaces based on their purpose.

The principle states that many client-specific interfaces are better than one general-purpose interface. Clients should not be forced to implement a function they do not need.

interface Worker { void work(); void sleep();
}

Let's say that we have 2 classes that implement Worker interface, Programmer and Robot class.

class Programmer implements Worker { public void work() { System.out.println("Programmer is working."); // Perform programming tasks } public void sleep() { System.out.println("Programmer is sleeping."); // Sleep at night }
} class Robot implements Worker { public void work() { System.out.println("Robot is working."); // Perform robotic tasks } // Can't be implemented public void sleep() { // Robots don't sleep! throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Robots don't sleep!"); }
}

As we see, sleep() method can be implemented by the Programmer class, but not by Robot class. To solve this, we can split Worker interface.

interface Worker { void work();
} interface LifeBeing { void sleep();
}

The Programmer class now implements all two interfaces, as it performs all the associated actions. However, the Robot class only implements the Worker interface, as it doesn't need to sleep.

class Programmer implements Worker, LifeBeing { public void work() { System.out.println("Programmer is working."); // Perform programming tasks } public void sleep() { System.out.println("Programmer is sleeping."); // Sleep at night }
} class Robot implements Worker { public void work() { System.out.println("Robot is working."); // Perform robotic tasks }
}

Dependency Inversion Principle

This principle refers to the decoupling of the software modules. Dependency inversion is a design principle in object-oriented programming that states that high-level modules should not depend on low-level modules. Instead, both should depend on abstractions.

// High-level module
class EmployeeTracker { private EmployeeService employeeService; public WeatherTracker(EmployeeService employeeService) { this.employeeService = employeeService; } public String getCurrentWeather() { int zipCode = 12345; return weatherService.getWeather(zipCode); }
} // Abstraction (interface) for employee service
interface EmployeeService { String getBranch(String employeeId); String getWage(String employeeId); String getPosition(String employeeId);
} // Low-level module implementing the employee service
class EmployeeServiceImpl implements EmployeeService { public String getBranch(String employeeId) { // Logic to retrieve employee data return employee.getBranch(); } public String getWage(String employeeId) { // Logic to retrieve employee data return employee.getWage(); } public String getPosition(String employeeId) { // Logic to retrieve employee data return employee.getPosition(); }
}

High-level module called EmployeeTracker needs to retrieve employee data. Instead of directly depending on the specific implementation, it depends on the EmployeeService abstraction/interface.

The EmployeeServiceImpl class is a low-level module that implements the EmployeeService interface. It contains the specific implementation details for retrieving employee data, such as making API calls.

EmployeeTracker class doesn't depend on these implementation details, promoting loose coupling and ensuring that the high-level module is not tightly coupled to specific low-level modules.

By applying this principle, we invert the dependency relationship between high-level and low-level modules. The high-level module depends on abstractions, while the low-level modules depend on those abstractions. This promotes modular and flexible code design.

Conclusion

That's all a short explanation of SOLID principles. You can boost your code quality by applying these principles to your code. There are several benefits we'll get by following these principles :

  • Maintainability: Easier code maintenance and modification.

  • Scalability: Ability to add new features without modifying existing code.

  • Testability: Facilitates effective unit testing and code verification.

  • Reusability: Creation of reusable and interchangeable components.

  • Flexibility: Greater adaptability and ability to switch implementations.

  • Readability and Understandability: Clear, organized code structure for easier comprehension and collaboration.
